reasons: Ollama supports local generation/chat, embeddings, tags/listing, and pull/download through its HTTP API
Crytex does not treat Ollama as a runtime LoRA adapter backend; use a baked Ollama model outside Crytex if needed
CUDA is managed by Ollama/host installation, so Crytex can preflight connectivity but cannot guarantee GPU placement
mistral.rs is Crytex's local GGUF text runtime and role LoRA application path
Embeddings/rerank stay delegated to ONNX or another dedicated backend
CUDA support depends on build features, driver/toolchain, model architecture, and fallback availability
ONNX is an embedding/rerank runtime in Crytex, not a text generation backend
FastEmbed presets can download/cache models; local ONNX directories are caller-provided
CUDA depends on ONNX Runtime execution provider availability
OpenAI-compatible HTTP backends support chat/completions, embeddings, and model listing when the provider implements those endpoints
Crytex cannot apply runtime LoRA adapters inside remote OpenAI-compatible providers
Download and CUDA placement are provider-side concerns
Anthropic Messages API supports text/chat generation through configured model ids
Crytex uses the configured Anthropic model id instead of relying on public model discovery
Anthropic does not provide Crytex embeddings, rerank, download, CUDA, or runtime LoRA adapter application

trtllm_future_module.decision: kept as future optional module; it is outside the production runtime matrix until the crate is moved under crates/ and receives CI/toolchain probes

doctor_checks: NVCC or runtime DLL visibility when CUDA features are enabled
cuda_preflight.failure_mode: doctor must report typed unsupported/partial CUDA status and CPU fallback, not panic
